Title: The OsBDR1-MPK3 module negatively regulates blast resistance by suppressing the jasmonate signaling and terpenoid biosynthesis pathway
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lectin receptor-like kinase with N-terminal lectin domain and C-terminal protein kinase domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Universally expressed in all rice tissues; rapidly induced in leaves after Magnaporthe oryzae infection from 12 h onward
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Universally expressed in all rice tissues; induced in leaves by M. oryzae infection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; promoter:GUS; subcellular localization; pull-down; BiFC; coimmunoprecipitation; in vitro kinase assay; phospho-immunoblot; disease assays; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defense against Magnaporthe oryzae; rice blast resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates jasmonate signaling and terpenoid biosynthesis, thereby reducing accumulation of antifungal metabolites and decreasing blast resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BDR1 is a membrane-localized RLK induced by blast fungus infection. 2. Silencing or knockout of BDR1 enhances broad-spectrum rice blast resistance, whereas overexpression increases susceptibility. 3. BDR1 directly interacts with and phosphorylates MPK3, promoting M. oryzae-induced MPK3 phosphorylation. 4. BDR1 suppresses JA pathway gene expression, JA/JA-Ile accumulation, and terpene synthase gene expression, especially TPS3 and TPS29.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused growth retardation with lower grain number per plant and lower 1,000-grain weight; knockout showed no significant abnormality in measured agronomic traits
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising gene-editing target for improving blast resistance because loss of function increases resistance without obvious agronomic penalty
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native BDR1 promoter (promoter:GUS); overexpression promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, Beijing Institute of Genomics Data Center GSA: CRA007058
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of MPK3 and indirectly suppresses JA-mediated defensive terpene biosynthesis; identified as a negative regulator rather than a biosynthetic enzyme

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Jasmonate biosynthetic enzyme, allene oxide cyclase family
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Allene oxide derived from 13-hydroperoxy linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes unstable allene oxide to form the jasmonate-pathway cyclopentenone intermediate in JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream intermediate for jasmonic acid and JA-Ile production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by M. oryzae; higher transcript levels in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (aoc-2); RT-qPCR; disease assay; metabolite context from JA measurements
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes rice defense and resistance to blast fungus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA biosynthesis and blast resistance; aoc mutant is more susceptible to M. oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC expression is induced by blast infection and elevated in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants. 2. aoc-2 mutants show increased susceptibility to M. oryzae. 3. AOC-dependent JA biosynthesis positively contributes to rice blast resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing AOC activity/expression could strengthen JA-mediated disease resistance, but broader JA-growth tradeoffs were not tested here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice genome encodes one copy of AOC according to the paper

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts monoterpene precursor pool into the volatile monoterpene linalool
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated by M. oryzae; higher in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants; lower in aoc and myc2 mutants after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; disease assay; antifungal assay with linalool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal volatile compound inhibiting M. oryzae growth and appressorium formation; contributes to blast resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively contributes to blast resistance by promoting linalool biosynthesis; tps3 mutants are more susceptible to M. oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS3 is induced by blast infection and is more strongly expressed in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants. 2. TPS3 expression depends positively on JA signaling because it is reduced in aoc and myc2 mutants. 3. TPS3 knockout increases susceptibility to blast fungus. 4. Exogenous linalool directly inhibits fungal growth and appressorium formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae infection (TMC-1); exogenous linalool treatment in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering disease-resistant rice or for stacking with JA-positive regulators to increase defensive volatile production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states TPS3 is responsible for linalool biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts sesquiterpene precursor pool into the volatile sesquiterpene caryophyllene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated by M. oryzae; higher in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants; lower in aoc and myc2 mutants after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; disease assay; antifungal assay with caryophyllene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal volatile compound inhibiting M. oryzae growth and appressorium formation; contributes to blast resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively contributes to blast resistance by promoting caryophyllene biosynthesis; tps29 mutants are more susceptible to M. oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS29 is induced by blast infection and is more strongly expressed in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants. 2. TPS29 expression depends positively on JA signaling because it is reduced in aoc and myc2 mutants. 3. TPS29 knockout increases susceptibility to blast fungus. 4. Exogenous caryophyllene inhibits fungal growth and appressorium formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae infection (TMC-1); exogenous caryophyllene treatment in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for metabolic engineering of antifungal volatile terpenes in rice
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper identifies TPS29 as the caryophyllene biosynthetic gene

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative signaling component linking BDR1 to suppression of JA signaling and terpene biosynthetic gene expression during blast infection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Magnaporthe oryzae infection; phosphorylation by BDR1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AOC; AOS1; AOS2; JAR1; MYC2; JAZ8; TPS3; TPS29
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates expression of JA biosynthetic/signaling genes and terpene synthase genes during blast infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation-dependent signaling repression of downstream defense metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated by blast-induced phosphorylation; JA and TPS target genes elevated in mpk3 mutants under infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; pull-down; BiFC; coimmunoprecipitation; in vitro kinase assay as substrate; phospho-immunoblot; RT-qPCR; disease assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of blast resistance by suppressing JA signaling and defensive terpene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPK3 directly interacts with BDR1 and is phosphorylated by BDR1. 2. Blast-induced MPK3 phosphorylation is reduced in bdr1 mutants and increased in BDR1-overexpression lines. 3. mpk3 mutants show enhanced resistance to M. oryzae. 4. JA pathway genes and TPS3/TPS29 are up-regulated in mpk3 mutants, showing MPK3 suppresses defense metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing MPK3 signaling in appropriate contexts may enhance blast resistance, though broader pleiotropic effects were not evaluated here
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context CRA007058; RT-qPCR; phosphoprotein assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MPK6 also interacts with BDR1 in vitro/in vivo, but BDR1-dependent in vivo phosphorylation effect was evident mainly for MPK3

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive JA-signaling regulator required for expression of defensive terpene synthase genes and blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signaling; negatively influenced by BDR1-MPK3 module during blast infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively regulates TPS3 and TPS29 expression according to mutant expression analyses; inferred direct regulation remains untested in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive terpenoid biosynthesis and defense response
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation within JA signaling pathway
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under M. oryzae treatment; transcript level elevated in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Linalool; caryophyllene; jasmonic acid-mediated defense outputs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA-mediated defense and terpene synthase expression; myc2 mutant is more susceptible to M. oryzae
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 transcript accumulates more strongly in bdr1 and mpk3 mutants under blast infection. 2. myc2-5 mutants are more susceptible to M. oryzae. 3. TPS3 and TPS29 transcript levels are reduced in myc2 mutants, indicating MYC2 positively regulates defensive terpene biosynthesis downstream of JA.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive target to boost JA-dependent defense metabolism, but broader JA-associated growth costs should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper infers possible direct binding to G-box motifs in TPS promoters based on prior studies, but direct binding was not tested here
